HGSS.138.0000:
This is the “Solidarity” room.\f
It is to show the solidarity of Pokémon
and people, as well as the history\r
of the Pokéathlon.
HGSS.138.0001:
Only the Pokéathletes who have
come in first in five of the courses\r
are allowed here.
HGSS.138.0002:
This is the “Trust” room.
Please go ahead.
HGSS.138.0003: The Pokéathlon does go way back.
HGSS.138.0004:
When you win a course,
you receive a Medal!\r
I’ve got to win at every course!\f
HGSS.138.0005:
I heard that Pokéathletes with great
records are going to have their shoes\r
displayed at the museum!
HGSS.138.0007:
It is an old wall painting.\f
One person and three Pokémon
seem to be learning something\r
from looking up at the sky.
HGSS.138.0008:
It is an old, worn scroll.\f
“Pokéathlon Myth\f
When a disease took over the entire
island, one human and three Pokémon\r
overcame ten obstacles to spread the\r
word about the cure.”
HGSS.138.0009:
It’s an old scroll.\f
“The Beginning of Pokéathlon\f
To commemorate the friendship of the
person and three Pokémon that\r
conveyed the words of the cure, people\r
started to tell of their achievement.”\f
...
The commemorating eventually took the\r
form of ten games to compete in, just\r
like the obstacles in the legend...”
HGSS.138.0013:
“Pokéathlon Almanac”\f
There are many Pokéathletes and
Pokémon Medalists shown in\r
the photos.
